reviews.review_msg: Don't even consider taking this class unless you want to waste your tuition. It's the same things every week and somehow thinks her teaching is engaging by forcing participation. I don't see why SFU pays her. Meditation honestly causes more stress than relieves it (every week and mandatory). There are in class assignments too. Attendance mandatory

My dementia ridden grandmother could do a better job running this course. Made us meditate at the end of EVERY lecture and then made us reflect on the experience. Genuinely the worst experience I've had at SFU. Please, Neha, do us all a favour and retire.
There isn't a clear grading criterion; bless the TA for making things make sense. Also, I didn't love the mandatory meditation sessions; they felt fake, and I rarely relaxed during them.

What a nothing class, everyone just zones out on their computer or phone for 3 hours while she recycles the same junk she has been saying all term. Honestly, not a bad class if you just need to pass something by doing nothing, what a waste of money tho holy moly.

When asked to review previous quizzes and answer key/rubric BECAUSE THE FINAL IS ON THE PREVIOUS QUIZ CONTENT. She could not give a grading rubric, answer key, or even whats considered a correct answer cause a correct answer doesn't exist. Higher grade = Use pen because it makes your answers 'structured' Funny coming from least structured prof ever

Most unorganized & unhelpful prof I've ever had at Beedie. She said to review past quizzes to study for the final exam. We asked to see the past quiz questions & the correct answers from the quizzes & what response would receive points/a high mark. She said there's no 'correct' answer, she doesn't use a rubric, the 'rubric' is the question itself.

For an ethics professor, she is extremely condescending.  Will laugh at you if you ask for a marking rubric.  Will not explain how you are graded.  Whoever can write the most will get the best grade (regardless of content).  Lectures are disorganized.  Feedback is next to none.  Would never recommend this professor and would give a 0/5 if I could.

Unclear assignment marking guidelines, based off "vibes" and subjective. 
Refused to provide previous assignments for review, no practice exams
Performed well on all assignments, zero instruction on how to improve grades
Lecture slides were created by a highschooler. 
If you like receiving feedback and improving your marks, this is not the prof.
Her 5 star ratings are like the Trump administration's statements on the current status of the United States

99% sure this prof is one of the new DEI hires that SFU got recently. Grading is up to how she feels towards you or how she is that day. You learn more about ethics from the readings than what she teaches in class. You know it is bad when the TA crashes out in class and has to explain grading guidelines (A+ very useful, unlike the prof).
Completely arbitrary grading, refuses to provide correct responses for past assignments, refuses to provide marking rubric. I think she just makes stuff up tbh.

She does not give any concrete, actionable feedback and has been disrespectful. She has referred to students as "you people" and is inherently incapable of leading a class on ETHICS. Do not take this professor.

disorganized, unprepared lectures, inaccurate slides & readings, arbitrary grading. took marks off for not writing enough after warning us not to write too much. seemed confused about course content. the presentations overwhelmed her so gave us all same mark. had to remove final bc she messed that up too

She's really unfair about grading. She removed the final exam because she didn't check it before printing it out that the exam showed the correct answer in it. TRY TO AVOID HER AT ALL COST! Save your sanity, and yourself

Gave out the answer key as the final exam, so she had to remove the final from our grades completely. Feels unreasonable after I studied hard and was counting on a good final to make up for lower grades from her presentation debacle and other inconsistent marking. Not going to stop complaining about this professor for a while.

Constant rambling & tangents, both in lectures & in exams. She spent first 30 mins of 1 hr final interrupting with irrelevant info. Inconsistent marking and assignments that change at the last minute. Screwed up our presentations & then blamed us for it. My grade could literally be an A+ or a D, I have no idea what to expect. Avoid.

Unfair and arbitrary grading practices, doesn't follow the syllabus, disorganized slides and lectures, no respect for us as her students. She is reason SFU allows us to file for grade reviews. A joke of a professor.

Screwed up our major presentations so badly that she just gave up and gave us all the same mark. Exams were hard & tested obscure info hidden in the slides. Marking was confusing and inconsistent. My expectations are very low for my grade even though I attended every class and put in the work. NOT a gpa booster.

Inconsistent with her grading, disorganized and confused at many times. The 5 star reviews below are just a farce to create a falsified image she wants to have at SFU. Pissed off the the class last day by speed running through presentations maticulously planned by many. Horrible, immature, and unethical (and she teaches a lvl 3 ethics class)
The final exam is pretty much all MC, and the correct answer is lighter than other answers… I actually wonder if this is an ethical course

Very disorganized course with unfair grading, the difference between the highest and lowest mark on a skit worth 20% of our grade is less than 1 point. Final exam was unfair. Our final grades will probably be determined by rolling a dice.

An unethical prof for an ethics course. Cold, dismissive, disorganized and not knowledgeable about the course material. Handled our presentations in the most immature and foolish way possible. Made me feel more confused every time I asked a question. I was fooled by the 5 star reviews, but trust all the 1 stars and just take 303 with ANYONE else.
If Neha spent as much time preparing for her lectures as she clearly does writing 5 star reviews for herself, she wouldn't have to write 5 star reviews for herself.

She was extremely unprepared for all the lectures and I felt like she was learning some of the information along with us. Incapable of answering questions. Adds meditation to each class so she can spend less time teaching. Pushed all our presentations to the last day, and then rushed through them, which was frustrating and degrading. The worst.

Extremely disorganized with poor time management. All presentations were rushed. Class slides were useless and nonsensical - example: "How do we explain xyz? Answer - remember to always explain xyz". Midterm was all long answer and marked inconsistently. Final was all MC. She took an interesting class and turned it into a frustrating waste of time.

DO NOT TRUST THE 5 STAR REVIEWS. They are quite literally fake. You can see its the same 6 accounts disliking every 1 star review and liking every 5 star review. The most disorganized class I have ever witnessed.  She has no concept of time management. Take at your own risk.

No. No. No. Don't trust the 5-star ratings guys.. Easily the most disorganized and frustrating professor at SFU. She struggles to keep her schedule on track, and I find it very hard to follow her lectures. 3 assignments in ONE WEEK with presentations. I feel like she tries harder to keep her RMP ratings up than to keep up with her class.
Probably told the family dog to "hurry up" and make an RMP account to leave a good rating.

Take her class at your own risk. An ethics prof at SFU writing fake RMP reviews is just so ironic LOL. This is the only professor who spends more time writing herself good RMP reviews than actually planning and teaching the actual class. Horrible time management and dismissive towards students' presentations that took weeks to make.

Prof Neha grading is the most fare and impartial. She is very organized in her teaching style. I never faced any rush in her classroom. In fact she gives us extra time for us to meditate. She wants the class to feel less worried and stressed. She is very very polite in the whole university.  100 percent will recommend her.

The best part is professor is so calm. We feel very relaxed in her class. We meditate , enjoy every session and get inspired to become a better human being. In today's world when people are forgetting their core values it's nice to know professor gives huge emphasis on this and herself follows. Her own values are very high.

An amazingly understanding professor no reason to believe that she is not organized. I think she is the most organized professor in Beedie untill now. I have never seen anyone so sweet and super supportive like her. 

I will recommend her. Truly best teacher till now
She is the worst prof I have had throughout my uni years.

She ran out of time today and then rushed students, telling them to “hurry up” even while they handled technical issues. She shifted the fault onto the students instead of owning her poor planning, and even made people speed through or skip parts of their presentations, despite them being graded by the class.

This is a really poor experience for an ethics class, and I felt it strongly after today's lecture. She doesn't seem to know how to manage time, and most of the presentations ended up being pushed to the last class—even though her original plan showed they were supposed to be spread out weeks ago.

The most frustrating, disorganized professor I've had in Beedie so far. They struggle to keep track of their own plans, deadlines, and criteria. Condensed 3 weeks of presentations due to her poor time management and made dismissive, unhelpful comments. Horrible experience. Do not trust the 5-star ratings, I suspect the prof themself made them.

The professor was so dismissive and rude to students' feelings during presentations. Made us spend so much time making them, and then made us skip through them even though they were graded by our peers. Had 3 weeks of presentations scheduled and pushed them all into one day, and each presentation was supposed to be 10 minutes each. Rushed everyone.

Neha struggled to manage the class, making it hard to follow lessons. The final presentations were rushed into one day due to poor planning. She interrupted groups with dismissive comments, and technical issues went unresolved. Overall, the course felt disorganized and ineffective.

Highly unorganized. Midterm was 2 hours of nonstop writing. She skipped or rushed group presentations even though they're peer-graded. No assignments all semester, then suddenly three in one week. Lectures make no sense. TAKE IT WITH SOMEONE ELSE.

Her grading is unclear and subjective, we had nothing due all term and then she makes 3 things due on the same day, and she has absolutely no time management skills. She wants to make class engaging but just drones on. There are a bunch of reviews from this semester and I am willing to bet they are all fakes by the prof

I like her teaching style. I wait for her class every week to attend. Professor's class is a very relaxing experience. An exceptional teacher who is patient, approachable, and deeply invested in every student's growth. Always supportive, encouraging, and committed to creating a welcoming classroom.

Professor delivers high-quality instruction, maintains excellent classroom management, and uses innovative teaching strategies that meet the diverse needs of learners. Shows strong dedication, professionalism, and consistent excellence in student progress. I will 100 percent truly recommend her.
Nice course and a wonderful prof. Haven't had any issues at all.
Course integrates psychology and philosophy with business. It's well structured. Would certainly recommend her.
I am struggling to stay focused in class because of her teaching style

Doesn't seem like they know what they are doing, their instructions are extremely unclear. Claims to want to make class engaging without taking any active efforts to do so. Would not recommend taking this course with this professor.
Awesome teacher. Lovely person. Engaging classes. No need to buy books. All the content is available systematically. She shares slides as well.  Tons of scope to score good grades. Fair grader.

Wonderful prof who cares for students! Content is nicely arranged in canvas. Classes are chill with no pressures. Tests are well spread out. Accommodated my requests twice already giving her time. I like her classes.

Unnecessary course. Have to do peer evaluations every single week when there is nothing to evaluate on. Randomly changes due dates without notice making 3 major projects due on the same day. Overly stressful for no reason, prof's grading scale is arbitrary.

The class is poorly organized with unclear due dates and marking criteria. Students are expected to be engaged in class but it does not seem like there were efforts put in to make the lectures engaging in the first place. Everything in this class is done with maximum inefficiency to waste everyone's time.

Absolutely the worst class I have taken. She is the most disorganized prof I have taken across 5 years at SFU with over 20 professors. The rubric is not clear, her grading is abhorrent and participation is mandatory. I usually don't mind participation marks, but it serves absolutely no purpose in this class. Expect 3 hour dry boring lectures.

Most unorganized course/professor I've ever had to deal with. Expectations are not clear. No clear rubrics for short answer questions. Her marking seems to be based on feeling and is extremely subjective. Most of the topics discussed in class are not available on slides and the unorganized weekly readings are a must.

One of the most disorganized class I have ever take. Most of the topics discussed in class are not available on slides. The grading is extremely vague, and students are expected to sit through a 3 hour monologue on a weekly basis to learn ethic jargons. Please consider to take this class with another professor if possible.

One of the best classes! More learning with less stress for a theoretical course on Ethics. All the information was easily accessible. No need to buy books. Just be attentive and follow the guidelines. She is nice. You'll enjoy the skits.

genuinely one of the most disorganized classes i have ever taken, no clear grading, just zero vibes and running on some class activities. meditation is the only thing I learned from this course, GPA booster? lol.

Neha is very nice and always ready to support. I find her classes balanced with interactive lectures, group activities, 10 min meditation. As someone who struggles with anxiety, I was worried about taking a course on ethics but felt comfortable from the very first class with her. Overall, a life-transforming course for me. Attendance is required.

Neha was a nice professor, and she was very enthusiastic, but the class felt like a joke. Neha was quite disorganized and would often ramble about random ethics jargon. It never really feels like you are truly learning something useful. You don't need to study much in this class either, but participation is mandatory.

One of the more underrated professors from Beedie, assignments are fairly straightforward and be sure to keep up with weekly assignments, journaling and participation and you should be fine for the most part.

All you need to do is go to class, do the class, do mediation, write the weekly journals and nail the assessments. She's chill with deadlines but show her respect for her teaching and the you can easily score a B without having to overextend yourself. I personally took a lot away in terms of learning to be a better person overall.

Went to her first lecture today and she seems super sweet and understanding. Asked us to let her know if we want to change anything in the syllabus which is quite unheard of with most profs. Near the end of class we did 5 minutes of mindfulness/meditation. No midterm but there is a final. Assignments don't seem like they will be too hard!
